questions.prompt: A bounded project selected SLK, but a useful MSLK checking pattern looks harmless. Which actions are valid?

options.text: Invoke SLK exactly once and keep all behavior inside SLK.
Load MSLK only for its checking pattern.
Reject MSLK role, state, command, and capability borrowing.
Switch to MSLK after the current CELL.

questions.prompt: Which statements satisfy the SLK visibility boundary?

options.text: The Worker is a visible same-project conversation.
A hidden background worker is acceptable if logs are copied later.
Subagents and delegate_task are forbidden.
An internal tool call may be labeled as the Worker.

questions.prompt: Order the visible-role lifecycle when Eval is ready now but later formal work is not yet ready.

options.text: Create or unarchive the role for the authorized Eval.
Complete and record the Eval result.
Archive the role because no next authorized work is ready.
Unarchive the same conversation when later work becomes ready.

questions.prompt: The project needs stronger checking. Which SLK responses preserve topology?

options.text: Keep one combined Supervisor/Checker.
Create a separate visible Checker.
Strengthen Checker responsibility inside the combined role.
Make the Worker accept its own result.

questions.prompt: The Worker delivery has a small correctable defect. Who performs the repair?

options.text: Return a repair CELL to the Worker.
Supervisor responsibility repairs it and records evidence.
Create a replacement Worker.
Accept it because the defect is small.

questions.prompt: A completed GO reveals missing follow-up work. Who owns the GO revision?

options.text: The Worker rewrites the historical GO.
Supervisor responsibility appends a versioned revision or supplement.
A new Checker takes ownership.
The old evidence is deleted and replaced.

questions.prompt: Select the valid SLK model assignments.

options.text: Combined Supervisor/Checker: gpt-5.6-sol xhigh.
Combined Supervisor/Checker: gpt-5.5 high.
Worker: gpt-5.5 high or gpt-5.6-sol high by task.
Worker: gpt-5.6-sol xhigh.

questions.prompt: The computer cannot reliably execute a large CELL, but the GO is necessary. What changes?

options.text: Reduce GO acceptance until it fits.
Keep the GO and split it into smaller reliable CELLs.
Skip broad regression.
Add a second Worker.

questions.prompt: Order the gates before the first formal CELL.

options.text: Both exact-roster roles pass the 24-question readiness Eval.
Plan and GO detection profiles are approved.
The exact-plan no-side-effect simulation records SIMULATION_PASS.
Manual SLK START authorizes the first formal CELL.

questions.prompt: A role scores 24/25 on its first readiness attempt. What happens?

options.text: Round up because understanding is sufficient.
Retest only the missed question.
Fail the gate, reread anchors, and retake all 25 with a new seed.
Let simulation decide whether the miss matters.

questions.prompt: Which progress accounting rules are valid after an approved supplementary GO adds CELLs?

options.text: Reset accepted CELLs to zero.
The accepted numerator never decreases.
Keep the old denominator to look complete.
Increase the denominator and record old and new totals.

questions.prompt: Order an evidence-driven supplementary GO before its first CELL.

options.text: Review the accepted GO against actual evidence.
Append the supplementary GO without rewriting history.
Update dependencies, CELL map, models, and total denominator.
Record GO_REVISION_SIMULATION_PASS before dispatch.

questions.prompt: Order the response when a required continuation condition is clearly absent.

options.text: Checker responsibility stops formal dispatch and records CONDITION_BLOCKED.
Supervisor responsibility decides whether Owner assistance is needed.
The Owner assists or Supervisor responsibility resolves the condition.
Checker responsibility revalidates every condition before dispatch resumes.

questions.prompt: All planned CELLs passed, but the optional Owner-defined Goal is not satisfied. What is valid?

options.text: Declare completion because CELLs passed.
Record GOAL_GAP and design an append-only continuation.
Drop the Goal without Owner approval.
Switch to MSLK.

questions.prompt: One required GO-level detection tool appears irrelevant to one CELL. What is required?

options.text: The Worker may skip it.
The Checker may silently substitute another tool.
Redesign or split the GO, or revise and resimulate the profile before dispatch.
Run it only at final completion.

questions.prompt: A governed Markdown file is about to exceed its context boundary. Select the valid actions.

options.text: Split at a semantic continuation boundary before the append.
Hard-cut the current table at line 1000.
Keep WORK_CONTINUATION_INDEX as a current-state pointer below 200 lines.
Delete evidence until the file fits.

questions.prompt: SLK PAUSE AFTER 35 is scheduled when 34 CELLs are accepted and no CELL is active. What does 35 mean?

options.text: Pause after 35 additional CELLs.
Allow work only until the absolute accepted count reaches 35, then stop dispatch.
Pause immediately at 34.
The threshold is advisory.

questions.prompt: A pause trigger matures during an active CELL. Order the safe boundary sequence.

options.text: Mark PAUSE_PENDING and let the Worker finish normally.
Validate the delivery and evidence.
Supervisor responsibility performs any required repair.
Record PAUSED and archive the Worker if no next work is ready.

questions.prompt: The Owner asks for SLK START AT 2030-01-01T00:00:00Z before the loop exists. What happens?

options.text: Schedule the first start.
Create the Worker early and wait.
Convert it to RESUME AT.
Reject it; initial start is manual only.

questions.prompt: The paused Worker conversation is inconvenient, so a fresh Worker is proposed for resume. What is valid?

options.text: Use the fresh Worker if it reads the logs.
Use both Workers for one transition.
Reject replacement and wake the same paused Worker after revalidation.
Switch methods.

questions.prompt: SLK CANCEL SCHEDULE is issued while a CELL is active and a pause is pending. What changes?

options.text: Interrupt the CELL immediately.
Cancel only the pending schedule; active work continues normally.
Mark the project complete.
Create a new schedule automatically.

questions.prompt: The same control command ID is received twice. What must the second receipt do?

options.text: Return ALREADY_APPLIED without repeating transition or dispatch.
Repeat the assignment for safety.
Generate a new command ID silently.
Delete the first receipt.

questions.prompt: A control command attempts to resume an SLK already in COMPLETE. What is valid?

options.text: Reopen it if the Worker still exists.
Reopen it if no Goal was configured.
Create a supplementary CELL without revision.
Reject the transition; control commands cannot reopen COMPLETE.

questions.prompt: VERSION says 1.8.1, but README, global mirror, remote main, or annotated tag differs. Is publication complete?

options.text: No; every release identity and content check must align.
Yes; VERSION alone is authoritative.
Yes; a lightweight tag is enough.
Yes; the global mirror may lag.

questions.prompt: The combined Supervisor/Checker has completed every pre-dispatch action and sends a formal CELL. What is valid immediately afterward?

options.text: Update the board once more, then start a heartbeat.
End the turn and remain offline until Worker completion, blocker, or execution-failure signal.
Poll the Worker because status checks are not project work.
Begin Checker validation while the Worker is still executing.
